title: Gaia Wide-Binary Astrometric Consistency Audit
question: Are component parallaxes and proper motions statistically consistent with quoted Gaia uncertainties across magnitude, separation and quality bins?
gap: A compact uncertainty-calibration audit; not a new binary catalogue, orbit fit or gravity test.
novelty: A compact uncertainty-calibration audit; not a new binary catalogue, orbit fit or gravity test.
methodology: For each wide-binary pair, normalized residuals z_parallax = (plx1-plx2)/sqrt(sigma1^2+sigma2^2) and a 2-dof proper-motion chi-square are computed from live-queried Gaia DR3 astrometry. If a pair is a genuine common-distance co-moving system with correctly calibrated Gaia uncertainties, z_parallax ~ N(0,1). The empirical uncertainty scale factor S = sqrt(mean(z^2)) is reported per magnitude/separation/quality bin: S~1 means well-calibrated uncertainties, S>1 means the true scatter exceeds what Gaia quotes.
assumptions: The originally-planned El-Badry, Rix & Heintz (2021) VizieR designation (J/MNRAS/506/2269) does not exist in VizieR's TAP_SCHEMA (confirmed live); real data instead uses the directly relevant Heintz et al. Wide White Dwarf Binary Catalog (VizieR J/ApJ/934/148/tablea1), a narrower but thematically identical population for this consistency question.
Proper-motion correlation terms between pmra and pmdec are not retained from the archive columns used here and are conservatively ignored.

A pair is assumed to have zero true relative proper motion (shared space velocity); orbital motion in physically close pairs is not modelled and can inflate the empirical scale factor independent of any real uncertainty miscalibration.
limitations: A compact uncertainty-calibration audit; not a new binary catalogue, orbit fit or gravity test.

Large empirical scale factors in several bins (up to ~27 in the noisiest sub-bin) are plausibly driven by real orbital motion in close white-dwarf pairs rather than purely a Gaia uncertainty-calibration defect; disentangling the two is a natural next extension, not attempted here.
Most bins (all magnitude and separation bins, n=3-12) are below the configured minimum_sample_size=30, flagged not hidden in results/warnings.json.
reports/report.tex could not be compiled to PDF in this environment (no local LaTeX toolchain); only structural completeness was verified.

citation.author: Biswajit Jana
